mission.title: Install and Configure Shipwright
mission.description: Shipwright is a CNCF extensible framework for building container images on Kubernetes. It supports multiple build strategies (Kaniko, Buildpacks, BuildKit, Buildah) and integrates with Tekton Pipelines.

steps.title: Install Tekton Pipelines (prerequisite)
steps.description:
Shipwright requires Tekton Pipelines. Install it first.

```bash
kubectl apply --filename https://storage.googleapis.com/tekton-releases/pipeline/previous/v0.65.2/release.yaml
```

> Wait for Tekton pods to be ready before proceeding.

steps.title: Wait for Tekton to be ready
steps.description:
Verify Tekton Pipelines pods are running.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n tekton-pipelines --watch
```

> Wait until all pods show Running status.

steps.title: Install Shipwright Build
steps.description:
Install the Shipwright build controller.

```bash
kubectl apply --filename https://github.com/shipwright-io/build/releases/download/v0.19.2/release.yaml --server-side
```

steps.title: Install build strategies
steps.description:
Install the sample build strategies (Kaniko, Buildpacks, etc.).

```bash
kubectl apply --filename https://github.com/shipwright-io/build/releases/download/v0.19.2/sample-strategies.yaml --server-side
```

steps.title: Verify Shipwright is running
steps.description:
Check the Shipwright build controller is running.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n shipwright-build
```

> You should see the shipwright-build-controller pod in Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Shipwright
uninstall.description:
Remove Shipwright from your cluster.

```bash
kubectl delete --filename https://github.com/shipwright-io/build/releases/download/v0.19.2/release.yaml
```
